comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS+signed (round 1, --ref HEAD). Post-claim AC fixes (safe for close gate): corrected AC1's broken shell arithmetic to a clean [ -lt 800 ] test, and AC2 now greps all four engine symbols incl. _ReplayCtx (E6/E1 advisories). Result verified: _processors.py 652 LOC (was 832), _replay.py 217 LOC, no allowlist entry. This lands as the BASE of the session-provenance stack.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Implementing this epic's session-provenance fold (`_fold_claimed_session`, stories 68ef +
c557) added the claiming-session/harness fold to `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`, which was
already at EXACTLY the 800-LOC module-size soft cap on `main`. The additions pushed it to 832,
tripping the CI module-size gate. Rather than allowlist it (grow the grandfathered over-cap
set), split the file along the clean seam that already exists: the per-event **processors**
vs the two-pass **replay engine**.

## What
- **New module `src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py`** — move the replay ENGINE out of
  `_processors.py`: `scan_for_latest_snapshot`, `_ReplayCtx`, the flat `_EVENT_HANDLERS`
  dispatch table, and `replay_events`. This is a one-way dependency (the engine imports the
  `process_*` processors; the processors never call the engine), so it is a clean seam.
- **`_processors.py`** keeps ONLY the per-event `process_*` fold functions; drop the
  now-unused imports (`Callable`, `NamedTuple`, `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`, `TAG_DELTA` — all were
  used solely by the engine/handler-table). Result: `_processors.py` drops from 832 to ~652
  LOC, back under the 800 soft cap with no allowlist change.
- **Importers** — `reducer/_api.py` and `reducer/__init__.py` import `replay_events` /
  `scan_for_latest_snapshot` from `._replay` instead of `._processors` (and the `__init__`
  module docstring's provenance note is updated).
- **Test** — `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py::test_downgraded_clone_preserves_and_ignores`
  monkeypatches `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` / `TAG_DELTA`; retarget those patches from `_processors`
  to `_replay` (the module the replay loop now reads them from).
- Pure move — NO behavior change; the reducer's public API (`reduce_ticket`, `replay_events`,
  `scan_for_latest_snapshot`, the `process_*` functions) is unchanged.

## Scope
- IN: the file move (`_replay.py`), the `_processors.py` trim + import cleanup, the two
  importer updates, and the one test monkeypatch retarget.
- OUT: any behavior change; further reducer decomposition; the session-provenance feature
  itself (stories 68ef / c557 / 199b).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` is under the 800-LOC soft cap (no allowlist/ceilings
      entry added). Proof: `[ "$(wc -l < src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py)" -lt 800 ]` exits 0
      (the file prints fewer than 800 lines).
- [ ] `src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py` exists and holds all four engine symbols: `replay_events`,
      `scan_for_latest_snapshot`, `_ReplayCtx`, and `_EVENT_HANDLERS`. Proof: each of
      `grep -q "def replay_events"`, `grep -q "def scan_for_latest_snapshot"`,
      `grep -q "class _ReplayCtx"`, and `grep -q "_EVENT_HANDLERS"` on
      `src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py` succeeds.
- [ ] The public reducer API still imports and works: `replay_events` /
      `scan_for_latest_snapshot` resolve from `rebar.reducer`, and `reduce_ticket` is unchanged.
      Proof: `python -c "from rebar.reducer import replay_events, scan_for_latest_snapshot, reduce_ticket"`.
- [ ] The module-size CONTRACT test passes (no over-cap drift). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q`.
- [ ] The reducer test suite (incl. the retargeted downgraded-clone test) stays green. Proof:
      `pytest tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py -q` and the full
      `tests/scripts/reducer/` directory.

## Depends on
- (none — a mechanical extraction; lands as the BASE of the session-provenance stack so the
  fold-bearing commits stay under the cap.)
compiled_state.title: Refactor: extract reducer replay engine to _replay.py (module-size seam)
